name: overcoming-unwanted-intrusive-thoughts
description: Sally M. Winston and Martin N. Seif's 'Overcoming Unwanted Intrusive Thoughts: A CBT-Based Guide to Getting Over Frightening, Obsessive, or Disturbing Thoughts' — the definitive self-help guide for intrusive thoughts. Covers the types of intrusive thoughts, why fighting them makes them worse, how the brain creates them, and the proven CBT approach to changing your relationship with your thoughts.
useCase: Understanding intrusive thoughts, why suppression doesn't work, learning the CBT approach, distinguishing intrusive thoughts from dangerous impulses, and finding recovery.
knownRisks: Not a substitute for professional therapy in severe cases
ERP approaches can be initially distressing
Some intrusive thoughts may require medication consultation
Not all therapists are trained in this specific approach
